title: Crisis contact documentation
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Supports documenting triage, current self-harm or suicide risk, leaving risk, capacity for a decision to leave, prior psychiatric history, mental-state examination and referral or follow-up when performed.

source.title: Mental Health in Emergency Departments: A toolkit for improving care
source.jurisdiction: United Kingdom emergency medicine; UAE law and pathways govern local use
source.population: Adults and children presenting to emergency departments with mental illness, self-harm risk or mental-health crisis.
source.setting: Emergency mental-health triage, initial assessment, documentation, observation and liaison working.
source.exclusions: Exact for documenting clinician-performed mental-health triage, current risk, agitation, capacity to leave, prior psychiatric history, mental-state examination, safeguarding and liaison referral. It does not predict future suicide or authorise detention, restraint or treatment in the UAE.

exact_location.heading: Mental Health Triage and Initial Assessment

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting current presenting state, agitation, immediate safety, safeguarding, willingness to accept care, observation level and care-plan status; it explicitly warns triage is not a future-risk prediction tool.

exact_location.heading: Mental-health audit standards for the emergency department

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting triage, current self-harm or suicide risk, leaving risk, capacity for a decision to leave, prior psychiatric history, mental-state examination and referral or follow-up when performed.

assessment.text: Supports recording that suicidal ideation, intent, support and immediate risk were directly assessed by a competent clinician; it does not populate a negative, assign risk, or generate escalation.
assessment.rationale: Supports clinician-completed self-harm and suicide risk assessment for mental-health crisis contact; no decision is generated.

source.title: Depression in adults: treatment and management — Recommendations
source.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE mental-health, crisis, referral, and legal adaptation
source.population: Adults with possible or diagnosed depression; communication and carer involvement are qualified.
source.setting: Primary and mental healthcare recognition and assessment.
source.exclusions: Exact for documenting mood and interest screening, duration, course, function, social and interpersonal context, sleep, substances, medicines, loneliness and clinician-completed risk assessment. It does not diagnose depression or generate a referral or treatment.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports recording that suicidal ideation, intent, support and immediate risk were directly assessed by a competent clinician; it does not populate a negative, assign risk, or generate escalation.
